perturbation.original_text: Payment for Fund shares redeemed shall be made in accordance with this section using the NSCC's Fund/SERV System. Payment shall be in federal funds transmitted by the NSCC to the Separate Account's Settling Bank as designated by the Administrator, on the same Business Day the Fund or the underwriter receives notice of the redemption order from the Administrator provided that the Fund or the underwriter receives notice by 10:00 a.m. Eastern Time on such Business Day.
perturbation.changed_text: Payment for Fund shares redeemed shall be made in accordance with this section using the NSCC's Fund/SERV System. Payment shall be in federal funds transmitted by the NSCC to the Separate Account's Settling Bank as designated by the Administrator, within 30 Business Days after the Fund or the underwriter receives notice of the redemption order from the Administrator.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the payment timeframe for redeemed fund shares to within 30 business days contradicts the usual swift settlement practices required and could potentially violate regulations around timely payment of redemptions.
perturbation.law_explanation: Rule 22c-1 under the Investment Company Act of 1940 requires funds to process redemptions at a price based on the current net asset value. Delaying the payment for 30 business days contradicts the requirement for a prompt redemption at the current value, effectively denying the investor the immediate use of their funds at the determined NAV.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 17 CFR § 270.22c-1 - Pricing of redeemable securities for distribution, redemption and repurchase. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 270.22c-1 - Pricing of redeemable securities for distribution, redemption and repurchase. CFR prev | next § 270.22c-1 Pricing of redeemable securities for distribution , redemption and repurchase. (a) No registered investment company issuing any redeemable security, no person designated in such issuer 's prospectus as authorized to consummate transactions in any such security, and no principal underwriter of, or dealer in, any such security shall sell, redeem, or repurchase any such security except at a price based on the current net asset value of such security which is next computed after receipt of a tender of such security for redemption or of an order to purchase or sell such security: Provided, That: (1) This paragraph shall not prevent a sponsor of a unit investment trust (hereinafter referred to as the “Trust”) engaged exclusively in the business of investing in eligible trust securities (as defined in Rule 14a-3(b) ( 17 CFR 270.14a-3 (b))) from selling or repurchasing Trust units in a secondary market at a price based on the offering side evaluation of the eligible trust securities in the Trust's portfolio, determined at any time on the last business day of each week, effective for all sales made during the following week, if on the days that such sales or repurchases are made the sponsor receives a letter from a qualified evaluator stating, in its opinion, that: (i) In the case of repurchases, the current bid price is not higher than the offering side evaluation, computed on the last business day of the previous week; and (ii) In the case of resales, the offering side evaluation, computed as of the last business day of the previous week, is not more than one-half of one percent ($5.00 on a unit representing $1,00
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the payment timeframe for redeemed fund shares from 'the same Business Day' to 'within 30 Business Days'. This contradicts Rule 22c-1 under the Investment Company Act of 1940, which necessitates processing redemptions at a price based on the current net asset value. As stated in Scraped Law Snippet 2: 'no such security shall sell, redeem, or repurchase any such security except at a price based on the current net asset value of such security which is next computed after receipt of a tender of such security for redemption'. Delaying the payment by 30 business days undermines the investor's ability to access their funds promptly at the determined NAV, thus creating a legal contradiction as the changed text does not align with the immediate redemption requirements mandated by law.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall terminate (a) at the option of any party, upon 90 days' advance written notice to the other parties hereto; or (b) in the event of a material breach that has not been cured within ten days following a written notice of breach to the breaching party.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall terminate (a) at the option of any party, upon 10 days' advance written notice to the other parties hereto; or (b) in the event of a material breach that has not been cured within one day following a written notice of breach to the breaching party.
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the advance notice period for termination and the cure period for a material breach creates a conflict with standard commercial and contract law practices which often require a reasonable timeframe.
perturbation.law_explanation: Ohio Revised Code § 1301.106(A) states that whenever a contract provides for one party to have the right to require performance or assurance at will from the other party, the contract shall be interpreted to mean that the party with the right may only demand such performance or assurance if the party acts in good faith. A contract term that specifies the duration of the contract must be given a reasonable time to expire or the aggrieved party must be given reasonable notification before the contract expires.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Section 1301.106 - Ohio Revised Code | Ohio Laws Skip to main content Back To Top Top The Legislative Service Commission staff updates the Revised Code on an ongoing basis, as it completes its act review of enacted legislation. Updates may be slower during some times of the year, depending on the volume of enacted legislation. Section 1301.106 | Use of singular and plural; gender - UCC 1-106. Ohio Revised Code / Title 13 Commercial Transactions / Chapter 1301 General Provisions Effective: June 29, 2011 Latest Legislation: House Bill 9 - 129th General Assembly PDF: Download Authenticated PDF Use of singular and plural; gender [UCC 1-106] In Chapters 1301., 1302., 1303., 1304., 1305., 1307., 1308., 1309., and 1310. of the Revised Code, unless the statutory context otherwise requires: (A) Words in the singular number include the plural, and those in the plural include the singular; and (B) Words of any gender also refer to any other gender. Available Versions of this Section June 29, 2011 – House Bill 9 - 129th General Assembly [ View June 29, 2011 Version ]
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the advance notice for termination from 90 to 10 days and the cure period for a material breach from 10 to 1 day. The Perturbation Explanation states that these changes conflict with standard commercial and contract law practices, which often require a reasonable timeframe. The Law Explanation and the Perturbation Explanation suggest a contradiction because they indicate that contracts must allow a reasonable timeframe before expiring or requiring performance. The altered contract language's drastically reduced timeframes directly oppose this requirement of 'reasonable time,' creating an inconsistency that could make the contract unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Payment shall be in federal funds transmitted by wire to the Separate Account as designated by the Administrator, on the same Business Day the Fund or the underwriter receives notice of the redemption order from the Administrator provided that the Fund or the underwriter receives notice by 10:00 a.m. Eastern Time on such Business Day.
perturbation.changed_text: Payment shall be in company credit, delivered by mail, to the Separate Account as designated by the Administrator, within 60 Business Days of the Fund or the underwriter receiving notice of the redemption order from the Administrator.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the payment method to 'company credit delivered by mail' and extending the payment timeframe to 60 business days contradicts regulations and standards for prompt and appropriate redemption payments.
perturbation.law_explanation: Rule 22e-3(c) describes that the fund should deliver the proceeds of redemption to the redeeming shareholder within 7 calendar days after the tender. The change to 60 Business days is a direct contradiction of the rule.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 17 CFR § 270.22e-3 - Exemption for liquidation of money market funds. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 270.22e-3 - Exemption for liquidation of money market funds. CFR prev | next § 270.22e-3 Exemption for liquidation of money market funds. (a) Exemption. A registered open-end management investment company or series thereof (“fund”) that is regulated as a money market fund under § 270.2a-7 is exempt from the requirements of section 22(e) of the Act ( 15 U.S.C. 80a-22(e) ) if: (1) The fund , at the end of a business day , has invested less than ten percent of its total assets in weekly liquid assets or, in the case of a fund that is a government money market fund , as defined in § 270.2a-7(a)(14) or a retail money market fund , as defined in § 270.2a-7(a)(21) , the fund 's price per share as computed for the purpose of distribution , redemption and repurchase, rounded to the nearest one percent, has deviated from the stable price established by the board of directors or the fund 's board of directors, including a majority of directors who are not interested persons of the fund , determines that such a deviation is likely to occur; (2) The fund 's board of directors, including a majority of directors who are not interested persons of the fund , irrevocably has approved the liquidation of the fund ; and (3) The fund , prior to suspending redemptions, notifies the Commission of its decision to liquidate and suspend redemptions by electronic mail directed to the attention of the Director of the Division of Investment Management or the Director's designee. (b) Conduits. Any registered investment company, or series thereof, that owns, pursuant to section 12(d)(1)(E) of the Act ( 15 U.S.C. 80a-12(d)(1)(E) ), shares of a money market fund that has suspended redemptions of shares pursuant to paragraph (a) of this section also is exempt from the requirements of s
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts established legal precedents regarding the timeframe for redemption payments in investment funds. The original text specifies payment on the same business day, whereas the changed text extends this to 60 business days, and also changes the method of payment. The Law Explanation states Rule 22e-3(c) requires proceeds of redemption to be delivered within 7 calendar days. Scraped Law Snippet 2 mentions section 22(e) of the Act (15 U.S.C. 80a-22(e)), although it is in reference to money market funds, it indicates there are rules regarding fund redemptions and timelines. Thus, the modification introduces a clear contradiction of the legal requirement for prompt payment, making the terms unenforceable.
